speaker: ARTHUR
text: The wall is high, and yet will I leap down.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Good ground, be pitiful and hurt me not.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: There’s few or none do know me. If they did,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: This shipboy’s semblance hath disguised me quite.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: I am afraid, and yet I’ll venture it.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: If I get down and do not break my limbs,

speaker: ARTHUR
text: I’ll find a thousand shifts to get away.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: As good to die and go as die and stay.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: O me, my uncle’s spirit is in these stones.

speaker: ARTHUR
text: Heaven take my soul, and England keep my bones.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Lords, I will meet him at Saint Edmundsbury;

speaker: SALISBURY
text: It is our safety, and we must embrace

speaker: SALISBURY
text: This gentle offer of the perilous time.

speaker: PEMBROKE
text: Who brought that letter from the Cardinal?

speaker: SALISBURY
text: The Count Melun, a noble lord of France,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Whose private with me of the Dauphin’s love

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Is much more general than these lines import.

speaker: BIGOT
text: Tomorrow morning let us meet him, then.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Or rather then set forward, for ’twill be

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Two long days’ journey, lords, or ere we meet.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Once more today well met, distempered lords.

speaker: BASTARD
text: The King by me requests your presence straight.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: The King hath dispossessed himself of us.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: We will not line his thin bestainèd cloak

speaker: SALISBURY
text: With our pure honors, nor attend the foot

speaker: SALISBURY
text: That leaves the print of blood where’er it walks.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Return, and tell him so. We know the worst.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Whate’er you think, good words I think were best.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Our griefs and not our manners reason now.

speaker: BASTARD
text: But there is little reason in your grief.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Therefore ’twere reason you had manners now.

speaker: PEMBROKE
text: Sir, sir, impatience hath his privilege.

speaker: BASTARD
text: ’Tis true, to hurt his master, no man’s else.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: This is the prison.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: What is he lies here?

speaker: PEMBROKE
text: O Death, made proud with pure and princely beauty!

speaker: PEMBROKE
text: The Earth had not a hole to hide this deed.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Murder, as hating what himself hath done,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Doth lay it open to urge on revenge.

speaker: BIGOT
text: Or when he doomed this beauty to a grave,

speaker: BIGOT
text: Found it too precious-princely for a grave.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Sir Richard, what think you? You have beheld.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Or have you read or heard, or could you think,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Or do you almost think, although you see,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: That you do see? Could thought, without this object,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Form such another? This is the very top,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: The height, the crest, or crest unto the crest,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Of murder’s arms. This is the bloodiest shame,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: The wildest savagery, the vilest stroke

speaker: SALISBURY
text: That ever wall-eyed wrath or staring rage

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Presented to the tears of soft remorse.

speaker: PEMBROKE
text: All murders past do stand excused in this.

speaker: PEMBROKE
text: And this, so sole and so unmatchable,

speaker: PEMBROKE
text: Shall give a holiness, a purity,

speaker: PEMBROKE
text: To the yet unbegotten sin of times

speaker: PEMBROKE
text: And prove a deadly bloodshed but a jest,

speaker: PEMBROKE
text: Exampled by this heinous spectacle.

speaker: BASTARD
text: It is a damnèd and a bloody work,

speaker: BASTARD
text: The graceless action of a heavy hand,

speaker: BASTARD
text: If that it be the work of any hand.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: If that it be the work of any hand?

speaker: SALISBURY
text: We had a kind of light what would ensue.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: It is the shameful work of Hubert’s hand,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: The practice and the purpose of the King,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: From whose obedience I forbid my soul,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Kneeling before this ruin of sweet life

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And breathing to his breathless excellence

speaker: SALISBURY
text: The incense of a vow, a holy vow:

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Never to taste the pleasures of the world,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Never to be infected with delight,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Nor conversant with ease and idleness,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Till I have set a glory to this hand

speaker: SALISBURY
text: By giving it the worship of revenge.

speaker:
PEMBROKE
              ,
               
              BIGOT
text: Our souls religiously confirm thy words.

speaker: HUBERT
text: Lords, I am hot with haste in seeking you.

speaker: HUBERT
text: Arthur doth live; the King hath sent for you.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: O, he is bold and blushes not at death!—

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Avaunt, thou hateful villain, get thee gone!

speaker: HUBERT
text: I am no villain.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Must I rob the law?

speaker: BASTARD
text: Your sword is bright, sir. Put it up again.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Not till I sheathe it in a murderer’s skin.

speaker: HUBERT
text: Stand back, Lord Salisbury, stand back, I say.

speaker: HUBERT
text: By heaven, I think my sword’s as sharp as yours.

speaker: HUBERT
text: I would not have you, lord, forget yourself,

speaker: HUBERT
text: Nor tempt the danger of my true defense,

speaker: HUBERT
text: Lest I, by marking of your rage, forget

speaker: HUBERT
text: Your worth, your greatness, and nobility.

speaker: BIGOT
text: Out, dunghill! Dar’st thou brave a nobleman?

speaker: HUBERT
text: Not for my life. But yet I dare defend

speaker: HUBERT
text: My innocent life against an emperor.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Thou art a murderer.

speaker: HUBERT
text: Do not prove me so.

speaker: HUBERT
text: Yet I am none. Whose tongue soe’er speaks false,

speaker: HUBERT
text: Not truly speaks. Who speaks not truly, lies.

speaker: PEMBROKE
text: Cut him to pieces.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Keep the peace, I say.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Stand by, or I shall gall you, Faulconbridge.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Thou wert better gall the devil, Salisbury.

speaker: BASTARD
text: If thou but frown on me, or stir thy foot,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Or teach thy hasty spleen to do me shame,

speaker: BASTARD
text: I’ll strike thee dead. Put up thy sword betime,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Or I’ll so maul you and your toasting-iron

speaker: BASTARD
text: That you shall think the devil is come from hell.

speaker: BIGOT
text: What wilt thou do, renownèd Faulconbridge?

speaker: BIGOT
text: Second a villain and a murderer?

speaker: HUBERT
text: Lord Bigot, I am none.

speaker: BIGOT
text: Who killed this prince?

speaker: HUBERT
text: ’Tis not an hour since I left him well.

speaker: HUBERT
text: I honored him, I loved him, and will weep

speaker: HUBERT
text: My date of life out for his sweet life’s loss.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Trust not those cunning waters of his eyes,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: For villainy is not without such rheum,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And he, long traded in it, makes it seem

speaker: SALISBURY
text: like rivers of remorse and innocency.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Away with me, all you whose souls abhor

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Th’ uncleanly savors of a slaughterhouse,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: For I am stifled with this smell of sin.

speaker: BIGOT
text: Away, toward Bury, to the Dauphin there.

speaker: PEMBROKE
text: There, tell the King, he may inquire us out.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Here’s a good world! Knew you of this fair work?

speaker: BASTARD
text: Beyond the infinite and boundless reach

speaker: BASTARD
text: Of mercy, if thou didst this deed of death,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Art thou damned, Hubert.

speaker: HUBERT
text: Do but hear me, sir.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Ha! I’ll tell thee what.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Thou ’rt damned as black—nay, nothing is so black—

speaker: BASTARD
text: Thou art more deep damned than Prince Lucifer.

speaker: BASTARD
text: There is not yet so ugly a fiend of hell

speaker: BASTARD
text: As thou shalt be, if thou didst kill this child.

speaker: HUBERT
text: Upon my soul—

speaker: BASTARD
text: If thou didst but consent

speaker: BASTARD
text: To this most cruel act, do but despair,

speaker: BASTARD
text: And if thou want’st a cord, the smallest thread

speaker: BASTARD
text: That ever spider twisted from her womb

speaker: BASTARD
text: Will serve to strangle thee; a rush will be a beam

speaker: BASTARD
text: To hang thee on. Or wouldst thou drown thyself,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Put but a little water in a spoon

speaker: BASTARD
text: And it shall be as all the ocean,

speaker: BASTARD
text: Enough to stifle such a villain up.

speaker: BASTARD
text: I do suspect thee very grievously.

speaker: HUBERT
text: If I in act, consent, or sin of thought

speaker: HUBERT
text: Be guilty of the stealing that sweet breath

speaker: HUBERT
text: Which was embounded in this beauteous clay,

speaker: HUBERT
text: Let hell want pains enough to torture me.

speaker: HUBERT
text: I left him well.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Go, bear him in thine arms.

speaker: BASTARD
text: I am amazed, methinks, and lose my way

speaker: BASTARD
text: Among the thorns and dangers of this world.

speaker: BASTARD
text: How easy dost thou take all England up!

speaker: BASTARD
text: From forth this morsel of dead royalty,

speaker: BASTARD
text: The life, the right, and truth of all this realm

speaker: BASTARD
text: Is fled to heaven, and England now is left

speaker: BASTARD
text: To tug and scamble and to part by th’ teeth

speaker: BASTARD
text: The unowed interest of proud-swelling state.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Now for the bare-picked bone of majesty

speaker: BASTARD
text: Doth doggèd war bristle his angry crest

speaker: BASTARD
text: And snarleth in the gentle eyes of peace.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Now powers from home and discontents at home

speaker: BASTARD
text: Meet in one line, and vast confusion waits,

speaker: BASTARD
text: As doth a raven on a sick-fall’n beast,

speaker: BASTARD
text: The imminent decay of wrested pomp.

speaker: BASTARD
text: Now happy he whose cloak and cincture can

speaker: BASTARD
text: Hold out this tempest. Bear away that child,

speaker: BASTARD
text: And follow me with speed. I’ll to the King.

speaker: BASTARD
text: A thousand businesses are brief in hand,

speaker: BASTARD
text: And heaven itself doth frown upon the land.
